metadata.purpose: Canonical Wave 3 execution ledger and readiness audit.
notes: Wave 3 planning docs were audited against repository reality before execution began.
Wave 3 scope was narrowed to unfinished work only.
Tasks already owned and completed by Wave 2 were removed from the active Wave 3 execution map and recorded as satisfied prerequisites instead.
Several Wave 3 dependency labels were non-canonical lane names rather than executable tasks; those dependencies were repaired into exact repository-backed prerequisites.

laneDefinitions.name: Design Runtime Foundations
laneDefinitions.purpose: Create the boundary, capability, reliability, and consistency foundations required before higher-order Wave 3 generation and adaptive loops.

laneDefinitions.name: Nexus Core Product Experience & Surface Delivery
laneDefinitions.purpose: Own the live Nexus product shell, route/navigation surfaces, unified home dashboard, guided execution surfaces, first-user experience, and the backend-to-frontend bridge that turns canonical state into usable product screens.

laneDefinitions.name: Existing Business Intake & Diagnosis
laneDefinitions.purpose: Turn uploaded assets, repositories, live sites, and analytics exports into normalized current-state diagnosis and executable import-and-continue plans.

laneDefinitions.name: Persistence & Return Continuity
laneDefinitions.purpose: Replace memory-only user, project, and session continuity with durable persistence and reliable return-tomorrow product behavior.

laneDefinitions.name: Behavioral Analytics & User Truth
laneDefinitions.purpose: Replace heuristic analytics with durable history, true user attribution, and retention truth.

laneDefinitions.name: Autonomous Product Ops
laneDefinitions.purpose: Turn post-execution evidence into measurable feedback, goal evaluation, and adaptive optimization loops.

laneDefinitions.name: External Capability Fabric
laneDefinitions.purpose: Own the registry, credential binding, webhook intake, provider health, and external-tool bridges that let Nexus connect safely to repos, providers, and imported design assets.

laneDefinitions.name: Execution Surface / Real Execution
laneDefinitions.purpose: Turn canonical execution intent into real provider, IDE-agent, and artifact-producing execution with strict routing, session, invocation, and result truth.

laneDefinitions.name: Deployment Reality
laneDefinitions.purpose: Turn execution outputs into real deployment attempts and canonical deployment evidence instead of assuming release side effects.

laneDefinitions.name: Live Release Verification
laneDefinitions.purpose: Validate real post-deploy production health and release readiness before Wave 3 can claim live launch truth.

laneDefinitions.name: AI Generation Engine
laneDefinitions.purpose: Establish the request, provider, service, execution, and normalization pipeline for controlled AI-generated design proposals.

laneDefinitions.name: AI Observability & Safety Ops
laneDefinitions.purpose: Measure generation health, provider reliability, prompt-contract failures, and review acceptance so AI output is governed by runtime truth rather than hopeful tests.

laneDefinitions.name: Owner Creative Control Plane & Generated Surface Proof
laneDefinitions.purpose: Prove generated surfaces are renderable, accessible, on-brand, reviewable, editable, attributable, and safely integrated.

laneDefinitions.name: Growth & Monetization Expansion
laneDefinitions.purpose: Expand Wave 2 billing foundations into true self-serve monetization state and lifecycle actions.

externalSatisfiedPrerequisites.name: Build & Release System
externalSatisfiedPrerequisites.note: Wave 2 still carries unresolved build/deploy cost truth. Wave 3 billing expansion is scoped to self-serve state and lifecycle, not full build/deploy monetization truth.

phantomTasksRemoved.name: External Model Integration
phantomTasksRemoved.reason: Planning section only, not a canonical executable task.

phantomTasksRemoved.name: Nexus Product Go-To-Market
phantomTasksRemoved.reason: Planning section only, not a canonical executable task.

phantomTasksRemoved.name: Extensibility Framework
phantomTasksRemoved.reason: Planning section only, not a canonical executable task.

phantomTasksRemoved.name: Project Permission Matrix
phantomTasksRemoved.reason: Satisfied cross-wave prerequisite, not a Wave 3 task candidate.

phantomTasksRemoved.name: UI / UX Foundation
phantomTasksRemoved.reason: Satisfied cross-wave prerequisite, not a Wave 3 task candidate.

duplicateRepairs.reason: Already completed in Wave 2; retained as prerequisite rather than re-executed in Wave 3.
Already completed in Wave 2; Wave 3 keeps only the durable learned estimator follow-on.
Already completed in Wave 2; Wave 3 keeps only the true byUser upgrade.
Already completed in Wave 2; Wave 3 keeps only the durable history store follow-on.
Already completed in Wave 2; Wave 3 keeps only true retention follow-ons.
Already completed in Wave 2 under workspace compute usage tracking.

duplicateRepairs.removedTask: Create storage and artifact cost tracker
Create cost visibility API and dashboard model

duplicateRepairs.reason: Already completed in Wave 2; Wave 3 keeps only self-serve lifecycle expansion.
Merged into Create AI design service to avoid a duplicate orchestration split.
Merged into Define AI design response schema plus Create AI design provider adapter to avoid a synthetic contract-only split.
Merged into Create renderable design proposal normalizer.

duplicateRepairs.removedTask: Create design proposal schema and store
duplicateRepairs.reason: Merged into Create design proposal state integration and generated asset provenance recording.
Merged into design validation plus review/apply control-plane tasks.
Merged into preview, diff, and review handoff tasks.
Removed from initial Wave 3 canon; regeneration belongs after first safe end-to-end design proof exists.

dependencyRepairs.reason: Repository already exposes canonical observability-derived reliability inputs through context-builder.
Go-to-market is a planning lane label, not an executable prerequisite.

repairedTo: optional extension registry when it exists
dependencyRepairs.reason: Wave 3 must be able to start with internal capability truth instead of waiting on a non-canonical lane label.
The external integration label is not a task; the real executable dependency is a strict provider adapter contract.

dependencyRepairs.taskName: Create billing plan catalog and selection model
dependencyRepairs.reason: Wave 2 already ships enough billing state to start self-serve plan selection.

repairedFrom: Create system bottleneck detector (stale doc status)
dependencyRepairs.reason: The detector already exists in repo; only the consistency validator and evaluation schema remain unfinished.

dependencyRepairs.taskName: Nexus Core Product Experience & Surface Delivery
dependencyRepairs.reason: The repository already has fragmented screen/runtime primitives and isolated surface models, but Wave 3 had no canonical owner for composing them into the live Nexus product experience.
The repo has provider health and review primitives, but no canonical Wave 3 lane owned prompt failures, provider latency/failure metrics, generation success, or acceptance truth.

The repo already accepts files, links, and connected repositories, but Wave 3 had no canonical owner for converting imported business assets into diagnosis, executable tasks, and continue-from-current-state plans.
The repo already exposes persistence contracts and some durable analytics history, but Wave 3 had no canonical owner for durable users, projects, sessions, and true next-day continuity.

repairedFrom: provider primitives spread across backlog lanes
dependencyRepairs.reason: The repo already contains fragmented connector/runtime primitives, but Wave 3 had no canonical owner for binding credentials, inbound provider events, source-control connectivity, or cross-provider health into a coherent product-facing fabric.

proactiveAdditions.reason: Wave 3 lacked canonical ownership for the authenticated Nexus shell even though repo reality already includes auth screens, workspace navigation, companion surfaces, and owner-facing dashboards.
Backend state already reaches isolated views, but no Wave 3 task owns assembling the live authenticated product shell.

proactiveAdditions.taskName: Create Nexus navigation and route surface binder
proactiveAdditions.reason: Backend-to-frontend route binding exists only as scattered runtime/view models; Wave 3 needed an explicit bridge owner.
Repo reality has owner and analytics surfaces, but no canonical Wave 3 task owns the default home entry surface for an authenticated Nexus user.
Wave 3 needs a single owner for the daily work surface that binds companion, recommendations, monitoring, and task state into a real user-facing workspace.

proactiveAdditions.taskName: Create settings and profile surface model
proactiveAdditions.reason: Account/profile/settings state exists in fragments, but no Wave 3 task owns rendering it as a canonical Nexus product surface.

proactiveAdditions.taskName: Create AI control center and generated surface delivery binder
proactiveAdditions.reason: The plan previously generated proposals without assigning ownership for surfacing accepted/generated Nexus screens inside the product itself.
The repo has fragmented workspace summaries, owner dashboards, and analytics panels, but no single Wave 3 owner for the main logged-in wow-value home.

proactiveAdditions.taskName: Create today priorities and next action feed
proactiveAdditions.reason: A product-grade home needs an explicit owner for priorities today and next best actions instead of relying on scattered roadmap panels.

proactiveAdditions.taskName: Create owner visibility strip for home dashboard
proactiveAdditions.reason: Owner visibility exists in operational dashboards, but Wave 3 lacked a product-facing summary layer that brings it into the main home experience.
The repo can orchestrate tasks and show queues, but there was no canonical Wave 3 owner for a focused task-doing experience.

proactiveAdditions.taskName: Create task step flow and progress binder
proactiveAdditions.reason: Product-grade execution requires a step-by-step task flow bound to real progress state, not just background cycle status.
Approvals already exist in runtime state, but Wave 3 lacked a user-facing execution handoff surface when a task needs approval.
Wave 3 needed runtime truth for prompt failures, provider latency, generation success, review acceptance, and rejection causes.

proactiveAdditions.taskName: Create provider latency and failure tracker
proactiveAdditions.reason: Provider health primitives already exist, but Wave 3 had no canonical tracker for design-generation provider reliability.

proactiveAdditions.taskName: Create generation success and acceptance tracker
proactiveAdditions.reason: Generated output cannot be governed truthfully without measuring produced proposals, accepted proposals, rejected proposals, and owner/operator acceptance.
Prompt contract doctrine in the permanent orchestrator needs a concrete telemetry owner in the canonical execution map.

proactiveAdditions.taskName: Create AI generation review dashboard model
proactiveAdditions.reason: Wave 3 needs a surface that turns AI-generation telemetry into operator-visible truth instead of burying failures in low-level runtime state.
Wave 3 adds generated UI surfaces. Safe execution requires a canonical proof contract for accessibility, performance, brand, provenance, and renderability evidence.
Generated surfaces need accessibility proof before they can be marked green.

proactiveAdditions.taskName: Create generated surface performance budget validator
proactiveAdditions.reason: Wave 3 needs real render/performance protection, not only structural validation.
Generated surfaces must respect product tokens and positioning, not merely render.
Wave 3 needs explicit ownership/history for accepted generated assets and proposals.

proactiveAdditions.taskName: Create uploaded intake to scanner handoff
proactiveAdditions.reason: The repo already stores onboarding uploads and has a project scanner, but the bridge from imported assets to diagnostic scanning was still missing and blocks true import-and-continue onboarding.

proactiveAdditions.taskName: Create existing business asset normalization layer
proactiveAdditions.reason: Wave 3 needs a canonical intake layer that turns mixed files, links, repositories, and exports into normalized imported business evidence instead of treating them as loose attachments.

proactiveAdditions.taskName: Create repository import and codebase diagnosis bridge
proactiveAdditions.reason: Git connectivity and project scanning exist, but no canonical Wave 3 task owns turning connected repositories into a current-state diagnosis flow.

proactiveAdditions.taskName: Create live website ingestion and funnel diagnosis model
proactiveAdditions.reason: Wave 3 lacked any owner for crawling an existing business website and turning it into structural UX/funnel diagnosis.
Analytics dashboards exist, but there is no canonical owner for importing external analytics exports and converting them into trusted product/growth signals.

proactiveAdditions.taskName: Create imported asset task extraction module
proactiveAdditions.reason: Imported files, sites, analytics, and repositories must become executable tasks rather than passive reference material.
Wave 3 needs a product path for continuing from an existing business reality instead of restarting every user from a greenfield journey.

Wave 3 needs one canonical owner for converting validated learning, feedback, and optimization outputs into real canonical tasks with execution_order and dependency truth instead of sidecar roadmap suggestions.
Wave 3 needs a canonical contract for which user, session, project, and workspace records must survive restart and power true return-tomorrow continuity.
Signup/login exists, but user identity currently remains process-bound instead of being product-grade durable state.

proactiveAdditions.taskName: Create durable project and workspace store
proactiveAdditions.reason: Projects and workspaces need durable persistence beyond process memory for a real product experience.
Return-tomorrow continuity needs durable session truth instead of ephemeral in-memory auth/session state.
Wave 3 needs an explicit owner for restoring a user to the right project, screen, and next action after restart or next-day return.
Wave 3 needed a canonical owner for describing which external systems Nexus can bind to, under what auth modes, scopes, and operational guarantees.
Git connectivity exists in isolation, but no canonical Wave 3 task owns binding repository state into live Nexus import and continuation flows.
Encrypted credential storage exists, but Wave 3 still lacked a canonical secret-resolution owner needed for real provider connectivity.
Provider contracts and encrypted credentials exist separately, but Wave 3 had no canonical owner for binding usable provider credentials into runtime connectors safely.

proactiveAdditions.taskName: Create inbound provider webhook ingestion gateway
proactiveAdditions.reason: Provider events can only become product truth when external webhook deliveries are normalized into canonical inbound runtime state.

proactiveAdditions.taskName: Create external provider health and failover orchestrator
proactiveAdditions.reason: Provider degradation and recovery primitives exist, but no single Wave 3 owner governs connector-level degraded-mode behavior across imported external capabilities.
Wave 3 AI/design flows need a real bridge for imported design assets instead of relying on generic file uploads and Figma-like attachment heuristics.
Wave 3 needs a canonical action-routing contract before external execution can move from UI state into real provider actions.
Provider selection and execution-surface routing cannot remain implicit once Nexus starts dispatching real actions.
Wave 3 needs an owned bridge that turns canonical execution plans into real provider invocations instead of stopping at planning state.
Local and IDE-based coding executors need a strict contract before they can participate in controlled Wave 3 execution.
Wave 3 needs a canonical adapter for safe local coding-agent execution instead of ad hoc external agent assumptions.
Execution providers must expose normalized capabilities before Nexus can route or constrain real execution truthfully.
Real provider execution needs canonical session lifecycle ownership for resume, cancel, and cross-provider correlation.
External executor results must be normalized into one Nexus runtime shape before they can update state safely.
Wave 3 needs a canonical invocation envelope for real execution attempts, approval posture, and expected side effects.
Real execution must collect artifacts, diffs, logs, and previews as first-class evidence instead of side effects.
Wave 3 needs a single owned execution-result surface that downstream validation, deployment, and feedback loops can trust.
The execution path still lacks a canonical owner for turning prepared release artifacts into a real deployment action.
Deployment truth is incomplete without an owned evidence collector for endpoints, environment identifiers, and provider metadata.
Deployment output needs a canonical normalized result surface before release truth can be validated safely.
Wave 3 needs a post-deploy health gate that proves launch reality instead of assuming deployment success implies release readiness.
Users need a canonical launch-confirmation state that reflects real post-deploy evidence rather than provider optimism.
Release truth needs a canonical evaluator that combines validation, approvals, deployment, and production-health evidence.
